payload.answer: Recall prefers current, non-superseded memories. In this case, mem_new matched Sam from Goldman and is the active replacement record, so it was returned. mem_old was excluded because it has been superseded by mem_new, and showing it as current would risk presenting outdated information.
payload.decision: Recall returned the active, newer memory for Sam from Goldman and excluded the older one because it was marked superseded by the newer record.

entity_resolution.reason: The explanation concerns existing memory records for Sam and Goldman rather than creating or changing entity identity.

memory_cards.statement: mem_new is the current memory about Sam associated with Goldman, so it remained eligible for recall.
mem_old was filtered because it is superseded by mem_new and should not be presented as the current fact.

details: Preserved the distinction between current and superseded memory.

repair_options: Verify that mem_old has an explicit superseded_by link to mem_new.
Verify that recall filters records marked superseded or inactive.
If both memories appear, adjust ranking/filtering to suppress superseded facts from default recall.
If lineage is unclear, store explicit validity or status metadata to distinguish current from obsolete records.
